content: {'summary': 'Presidential power exercise follows established procedures including executive order issuance, appointment confirmation, treaty negotiation, and veto enactment.', 'key_points': ['Executive orders published in Federal Register, numbered sequentially, subject to OMB review', 'Appointments require Senate confirmation for PAS (Presidential Appointment with Senate) positions (~1,200 roles)', 'Treaty negotiation conducted by State Department, requires 2/3 Senate vote for ratification', 'Veto process: President returns bill to originating chamber within 10 days (excluding Sundays)', 'Pocket veto occurs when Congress adjourns during 10-day period', 'Congressional override requires 2/3 vote in both chambers'], 'sources': ['Administrative Procedure Act', 'Senate Executive Clerk procedures', 'Federal Register Act', 'Congressional Research Service reports on veto process'], 'related_fragments': [], 'last_updated': '2024-01-15', 'credibility_score': 0.98, 'philosophy_guard_passed': True, 'philosophy_guard_note': 'Descriptive claim about presidential systems; no normative advocacy regarding what should be'}
